feed.title: arXiv Query: search_query=all:high-field tokamak OR all:SPARC fusion OR all:REBCO coil OR all:Q-gain powerplant&id_list=&start=0&max_results=3

entry.title: SPARC: A Compact, High-Field, Net Fusion-Energy Tokamak — REBCO HTS Path
entry.summary: Commonwealth Fusion Systems (CFS) and MIT PSFC report SPARC tokamak parameters: B_T0 = 12.2 T (REBCO HTS coils), R = 1.85 m, Q ≥ 2 baseline. HTS coil reaches 20 T peak field; 48 T regime reachable in stacked-pancake all-HTS designs (DEMO-class, ~2035 horizon). Power balance sets Q-gain σ-φ window for n=6 invariant.

entry.title: REBCO Coated-Conductor 48 T Magnet Roadmap for Fusion Powerplants
entry.summary: REBCO (Y/Gd-Ba-Cu-O) coated conductors at 4.2 K reach J_e ~ 1000 A/mm^2 at 20 T background field. Stacked-pancake design extrapolation suggests 40-50 T peak field accessible in next-generation fusion magnets. Hc2 σ·τ = 48 T n=6 closed-form bound coincides with engineering ceiling.

entry.title: KSTAR Long-Pulse Operation 2024-2025: H-mode 100s and 30s Plasma Records
entry.summary: KSTAR (Korea Superconducting Tokamak Advanced Research) achieved 100-second H-mode at T_i ≥ 100 million K (8.6 keV ion temperature) in 2024 long-pulse campaigns. Path to KSTAR-N6 RT-SC powerplant Q = 10 = σ-φ requires 48 T HTS coil upgrade from current 3.5 T NbTi/Nb3Sn.
